recent_lessons: Evan Mobley PTS T15 missed narrowly (14 actual vs 15 threshold) in 35 minutes in a competitive game (DET won by 10). The FG_COLD step from wherever to T15 was correctly applied, and the H2H 4/4 at T15 offset was used to reach the 68% minimum floor. At 35 minutes in a competitive losing game, 14 PTS on a cold-shooting night is a genuine single-unit miss. The pick was at the absolute confidence floor (68%) with multiple headwinds acknowledged — this is the cleanest near-floor variance miss of the slate.

De'Aaron Fox AST T4 missed (actual 2, pick_value 4) in an SAS blowout win (133-95). Game-script is the primary mechanism: Fox played only 26 minutes in a 38-point blowout where SAS managed his load in the second half. Even with a 9/10 historical hit rate and H2H MIN 3/3 perfect record, AST props on the winning side of a blowout are structurally at risk due to early lead management, starters resting in Q4, and reduced half-court creation opportunities once the outcome is decided. The blowout penalty applied (-5pp) was insufficient to bring confidence below the pick threshold — it landed at 80%, still a pick.

Stephon Castle 3PM T1 missed (actual 0, pick_value 1) despite POR series 5/5 T1 and SAS being a 9.5-point home favorite. Post-game news confirms Castle attempted only 1 three-pointer and missed it. In a 38-point blowout win, SAS's offensive execution shifted heavily toward two-point scoring (Castle finished 6-10 overall with 9-9 FTs, scoring 21 PTS without needing perimeter volume). The blowout game-script compressed Castle's perimeter attempt volume to a single shot — not a miss due to shooting percentage variance, but due to near-zero attempt volume. The 3PM iron_floor concept assumes sufficient attempts, not just shooting ability.

Anthony Edwards PTS T15 missed (actual 12, pick_value 15) and AST T2 missed (actual 0, pick_value 2) in an SAS blowout win (133-95). Post-game news confirms Edwards played 24 minutes with the miss attributed to lopsided game flow. At pick time Edwards was QUESTIONABLE, a -5pp injury penalty was applied to PTS, and proj_min was set to 30 — but actual minutes were 24 in a blowout loss. The combined effect of QUESTIONABLE minutes uncertainty and a losing-side blowout game-script was not fully priced: a QUESTIONABLE player on the projected losing side in a game with a 9.5-point spread faces both a minutes floor uncertainty AND a blowout compression risk, and these two penalties should compound rather than be evaluated independently.

Harden AST T4 missed with 3 actual vs 4 threshold (3 assists in 37 minutes, 3-13 FG, 4 turnovers) — DET's defense actively disrupted his playmaking sequences in a game where his shooting collapsed entirely; the post-game recap confirms no injury, purely an ineffective night. The series diary for CLE-TOR documented a similar Harden G7 AST compression (3 AST) under defensive pressure in a blowout-adjacent context. The 'bulletproof AST' dossier label, while supported by within-series stability historically, has now produced two single-digit AST games in this postseason when the game script forces him into volume-shooter mode under DET's defensive structure.

Mobley PTS T10 missed by exactly 1 (9 actual vs 10 threshold) on an off shooting night in a competitive game — the post-game recap confirms no injury and normal 36 minutes, classifying this as variance on a pick where the tier walk had already stepped down conservatively from T15 due to the G1 series miss at T15. The pick was structurally sound.

Mobley REB T6 produced only 1 actual rebound in 36 minutes — a catastrophic miss that is 5 units below threshold in a competitive game with normal minutes. This is not explained by blowout (DET won by 10, labeled COMPETITIVE) or injury (36 min played, no exit). The severity of the miss (5 below threshold, not 1) suggests a game-specific matchup factor that the quant data did not capture — DET's interior rebounding dominance with Duren (10 REB) and Ausar Thompson (7 REB) concentrated all defensive glass work, leaving Mobley without positioning opportunities despite full minutes.

Karl-Anthony Towns PTS T10 missed (actual 8) due to foul trouble limiting him to 26 minutes — the post-game news explicitly confirms 'limited by foul trouble again.' The Analyst correctly identified the foul trouble risk in the tier_walk ('foul trouble risk' cited explicitly) and capped confidence at 72%, the floor. The KAT playoff STRONG CAUTION dossier was applied. The miss is attributable to foul trouble compressing minutes below the threshold needed to reach 10 points — a mechanism the system flagged but could not fully price in at the T10 tier after the FG_MARGIN_NEG mandatory step forced the tier down from T15.

Rudy Gobert REB T8 missed (actual 7, one short of threshold) in a CLOSE game (SAS won by 7). The post-game news confirms he played 31 minutes through a pre-existing left rib sprain with no exit or restriction. The Analyst correctly applied the career playoff REB CAUTION (worsening trajectory -6.7pp at T6, monotonic_down) and stepped from T10 to T8 on the 25th-percentile gate. The miss by exactly 1 rebound in 31 minutes with a confirmed rib sprain is at the boundary of variance and a minor structural mechanism — the rib sprain was a known pre-existing condition that may have slightly reduced his physicality on contested boards without causing an exit. The Analyst's 72% confidence (floor) appropriately reflected this stack of risk factors.

Jalen Duren REB T8 missed badly (4 actual vs 8 threshold, -4 margin) in a CLOSE game (DET lost by 7) with no confirmed injury exit and full 29 minutes played. The post-game news explicitly confirms 'a simply poor rebounding performance.' The competitive=66% signal in the tier_walk was the only structural warning present — the analyst noted this as 'a significant concern' but proceeded to 78% confidence after a net adjustment. The miss highlights that competitive=66% on a REB pick, even with strong series form (2/2 T8), warrants a more aggressive confidence reduction or tier step than was applied.
recent_reinforcements: FG_MARGIN_THIN step-down cascade fired correctly on Maxey PTS (T20→T15), Castle PTS (T15→T10), and Randle PTS (T15→T10) — all three stepped picks hit, confirming the cascade rule is well-calibrated and should be applied without exception when FG_MARGIN_THIN is flagged.

Wembanyama blowout exemption is validated: his PTS T15 (actual 39) and REB T10 (actual 15) both hit in a CLOSE game (SAS won by 7), confirming that the structural floor holds in competitive playoff road contexts. The tier walk's decision to hold at T15 and T10 despite series underperformance in G2 (a blowout SAS win where he was managed) was correct — the G2 data point was context-contaminated, not a real signal.

Iron_floor 3PM picks went 2/2 (Brunson T1, Vassell T1): the iron_floor designation on 3PM provides reliable protection at T1, and the 80% confidence cap applied to both was appropriate. Both series-level hit rates (2/2 and 2/2 respectively) corroborated the pick. The top_pick designation on iron_floor 3PM picks is functioning correctly.

The lineup amendment agent correctly identified upside from OG Anunoby's absence for Maxey PTS (revised to 84%), Maxey 3PM (revised to 85%), Edgecombe PTS (revised to 84%), and Bridges PTS (revised to 76%) — all four hit. The Edwards QUESTIONABLE update correctly flagged upside for Wembanyama PTS (revised to 82%), Castle PTS (revised to 78%), Randle PTS (revised to 77%), and McDaniels PTS (revised to 79%) — all four hit. The amendment agent is adding genuine predictive value on lineup disruptions.

The 70-75% confidence band outperformed expectation at 81.8% actual vs 72.5% expected (9/11 hits), and the 76-80% band went perfect (8/8). This confirms the system's confidence calibration is conservative — picks at these bands are being selected at thresholds that consistently produce outcomes above their stated probability, which is the correct asymmetry for a prop-selection system.

Tier step-down discipline on FG_MARGIN_THIN worked across all four triggered instances (Holmgren PTS T20→T10, Cunningham PTS T20→T15, Mitchell PTS T20→T15, Harden PTS T15→T10): every stepped pick hit, validating that the mandatory step-down rule correctly converts borderline primary tiers into reliable floor picks without sacrificing viability.

The Mobley AST CAUTION directive (avoid T4+ in playoffs, T2 acceptable) was correctly applied — Mobley posted 4 AST vs the T2 threshold, hitting comfortably. The carry-forward of the career playoff AST CAUTION dossier signal produced a structurally sound pick that required no hedging.

Cunningham AST T6 selection anchored on his elite series average (8.5 AST) and the raw_avgs ≥6.0 primary-playmaker exemption produced a 10-AST actual, validating the exemption logic — his G3 playmaking was not suppressed by DET's own secondary-creator compression signal, confirming the dossier's primary-creator carve-out is correctly scoped.

LeBron AST T6 at 70% confidence in the face of multiple stacked penalties (VOLATILE+AST -8pp, LOAD RISK -5pp, competitive drop to 73%) still hit with 8 actual assists — the 70% floor confidence minimum correctly kept this pick alive despite a heavy penalty stack, and the pick contributed positively to the slate.

Ausar Thompson REB T6 top pick designation (80% confidence, consistent tag, 9/10 hit rate, H2H vs CLE T6 5/6) produced 7 actual rebounds in a CLOSE game (CLE won by 7), validating the top pick elevation logic for players with consistent tags and strong cross-series rebounding floors. The market edge (+8.57% vs FanDuel implied) was correctly identified.
recent_recommendations: KAT foul-trouble PTS skip rule: When KAT has accumulated 2+ fouls in Q1 or 3+ fouls in Q2 in prior series games (a pattern confirmed by the series diary and 'limited by foul trouble again' in both G2 and G3), treat any KAT PTS pick as foul-contingent — require live confirmation of foul pace before including in parlay. At pre-game selection, if the series diary documents prior foul trouble events, apply an additional -8pp confidence penalty on top of existing suppressors. At 72% floor with STRONG CAUTION and foul history, the system should consider a skip directive rather than forcing the pick to the floor.

Gobert REB CAUTION operationalization: The career playoff REB CAUTION (monotonic_down trajectory, -6.7pp at T6) fired correctly but the 72% floor kept the pick in. When the career playoff REB CAUTION is rated STRONG and the trajectory is monotonic_down, AND the player has a known soft-tissue injury (rib sprain, calf strain, etc.) active during the game, apply an additional -5pp to the already-penalized confidence. This would have taken Gobert from 72% to 67%, below floor — producing a correct skip. The combination of worsening cross-season trajectory + active soft-tissue injury is a compound structural signal that the current penalty stack does not fully price.

Pre-existing injury penalty for REB/PTS props: The Gobert miss highlights a gap — the system's injury penalty framework (QUESTIONABLE = -5pp on shooting props) does not have a defined penalty for known soft-tissue injuries that do not prevent play but may reduce physicality. Introduce an 'active_soft_tissue_injury' flag (-3pp on REB props, -2pp on PTS) when a player is confirmed playing through a rib, calf, hamstring, or similar non-structural injury. This would apply specifically to non-exit injuries confirmed in injury reports before tip-off.

FG_MARGIN_NEG + STRONG_CAUTION stack at minimum tier: The KAT PTS pick reached T10 only via the FG_MARGIN_NEG mandatory cascade (T15→T10). At T10 — the minimum PTS tier — the system has no further step available. When a mandatory cascade lands a pick at T10 AND the player carries a STRONG CAUTION dossier signal (KAT playoff PTS suppression rated STRONG) AND prior series games document a non-scoring mechanism (foul trouble), the system should treat T10 as a skip-eligible outcome from the cascade rather than a guaranteed pick. Add a rule: 'If FG_MARGIN_NEG cascade terminates at T10 (minimum tier) AND a STRONG CAUTION is active on that stat AND a non-scoring floor mechanism (foul trouble, minutes restriction) is documented in the series diary — skip.'

Series diary foul-trouble tracking for KAT and other foul-prone bigs: The series diaries for NYK vs PHI document KAT's minutes restriction multiple times ('limited by foul trouble'). The pick system should treat repeated series diary mentions of foul trouble as a structural series pattern — not individual-game noise — and weight them as a -5pp per confirmed occurrence penalty on any pick that depends on KAT playing 28+ minutes. This is distinct from the VOLATILE tag and should apply as an additive series-specific adjustment.

For REB picks where competitive hit rate drops materially below 70% (e.g. competitive=66% as seen with Duren REB), apply a mandatory additional -8pp penalty rather than treating it as a soft adjustment. If the resulting confidence falls below 72%, step the tier down one level regardless of series form. Series form (2/2) should not fully offset a structural competitive-game suppression signal — the two signals address different sample populations and the competitive sample is more directly relevant to playoff game scripts.

When a REB pick's 25th-percentile gate analysis finds the 3rd-lowest recent game value is close to or at the pick threshold (e.g. PHX series avg 9.4 with estimated low games near 7-8), and the competitive hit rate is simultaneously below 70%, treat this as a compound gate signal and step down one additional tier even if the 25th-pct gate nominally clears. The Duren T8 miss with competitive=66% and a PHX series that had borderline 3rd-lowest values illustrates that narrow gate clearances combined with poor competitive rates create outsized tail risk.

The obs_walk 'declined' outcome on Duren REB (T10→T8 declined, no T5 market) identified a structural limitation: when no lower market tier exists and FG_MARGIN_THIN fires, the analyst correctly held with a confidence penalty, but the combination of FG_MARGIN_THIN + FG_COLD (-21%) + competitive=66% on a REB pick with no walk-down option available should trigger a skip consideration rather than a held pick at floor confidence. Codify: if FG_COLD ≥15% AND competitive<70% AND no lower market tier exists for a REB pick, classify as skip-eligible at the analyst's discretion.

The Harden PTS dossier advisory ('AVOID PTS in playoffs') was correctly applied as a -10pp penalty rather than a hard skip, and the pick hit (19 actual vs T10). This validates the current treatment — dossier AVOID signals on PTS should continue to be operationalized as confidence penalties at conservative tiers (T10 and below) rather than absolute skips when series evidence (2/2 T10) and H2H data (3/3 vs DET) provide strong override context. Do not escalate the AVOID directive to a hard skip rule based on this slate.

The walk-revert outcome on Harden AST (T2 market unavailable, reverted to T4 with -3pp penalty) produced a 7-actual vs T4 hit. When a walk-revert occurs due to market unavailability, the -3pp penalty-in-lieu approach is functioning correctly — no rule change needed. Reinforce that walk-revert picks should continue to apply the -3pp penalty and remain in the slate, as the reversion to a higher tier with a small confidence haircut correctly reflects the incremental risk of not fully walking down.
Playoff calibration divergence in 86+% band: 60.0% actual vs 87.0% regular-season (-27.0pp, n=5). Investigate whether this is structural (playoff defense, scheme adaptation) or variance.
